 {"id":"bd-otf4","title":"Code Review: PR #481 - Context Engineering Optimizations","description":"Comprehensive code review of the merged context engineering PR (PR #481) that reduces MCP context usage by 80-90%.

## Summary
The PR successfully implements lazy tool schema loading and minimal issue models to reduce context window usage. Overall implementation is solid and well-tested.

## Positive Findings
✅ Well-designed models (IssueMinimal, CompactedResult)
✅ Comprehensive test coverage (28 tests, all passing)
✅ Clear documentation and comments
✅ Backward compatibility preserved (show() still returns full Issue)
✅ Sensible defaults (COMPACTION_THRESHOLD=20, PREVIEW_COUNT=5)
✅ Tool catalog complete with all 15 tools documented

## Issues Identified
See linked issues for specific followup tasks.

## Context Engineering Architecture
- discover_tools(): List tool names only (~500 bytes vs ~15KB)
- get_tool_info(name): Get specific tool details on-demand
- IssueMinimal: Lightweight model for list views (~80 bytes vs ~400 bytes)
- CompactedResult: Auto-compacts results with >20 issues
- _to_minimal(): Conversion function (efficient, no N+1 issues)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-14T14:24:13.523532-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-29T13:40:29.726141-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-29T13:40:29.726141-08:00","close_reason":"Stale/spurious - test artifacts, merged PRs, or auto-close candidates"}

 {"id":"bd-oxgi","title":"Add mol_type schema field to beads","description":"Add mol_type field to distinguish molecule types (swarm/patrol/work).

## Context
Swarms need to be distinguishable from regular work molecules. The mol_type field enables:
- Filtering: bd list --type=swarm 
- Queries: Find all active swarm molecules
- Coordinator logic: Different handling for different mol types

## Implementation
1. Add mol_type column to SQLite schema (nullable, default null = work)
2. Add mol_type to JSONL format
3. Update create command to accept --mol-type
4. Update list/ready commands to filter by mol_type
5. Values: 'swarm', 'patrol', 'work', null (null = work)

## Reference
~/gt/docs/swarm-architecture.md - 'Data Model' section

## Acceptance
- bd create --mol-type=swarm works
- bd list --mol-type=swarm filters correctly
- Existing molecules work unchanged (backward compatible)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-28T19:11:07.616818-08:00","created_by":"mayor","updated_at":"2025-12-28T19:50:33.213538-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-28T19:50:33.213538-08:00"}

 {"id":"bd-s00m.4","title":"Migration: Convert pinned work items to hooked","description":"Migrate existing pinned beads that represent work-on-hook to use 'hooked' status.

## Migration Logic

```sql
UPDATE issues 
SET status = 'hooked'
WHERE status = 'pinned' 
  AND issue_type NOT IN ('agent', 'role')
```

## What Stays Pinned

- Agent identity beads (gt-mayor, gt-witness-gastown, etc.)
- Role definition beads (gt-mayor-role, etc.)

## What Becomes Hooked

- Molecules on hooks (mol_type field set)
- Regular issues/tasks that were pinned to hooks

## Verification

After migration:
- bd list --status=pinned should only show agents/roles
- bd list --status=hooked should show work on hooks","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-28T22:17:13.872171-08:00","created_by":"stevey","updated_at":"2025-12-28T22:36:09.039612-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-28T22:36:09.039612-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-s00m.4","depends_on_id":"bd-s00m","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-28T22:17:13.872786-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-s00m.4","depends_on_id":"bd-s00m.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-28T22:17:22.168228-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"bd-sal9","title":"bd mol current: soft cursor showing current/next step","description":"Add bd mol current command for molecule navigation orientation.

## Usage

bd mol current [mol-id]

If mol-id given, show status for that molecule.
If not given, infer from in_progress issues assigned to current agent.

## Output

You're working on molecule gt-abc (Feature X)

  [done] gt-abc.1: Design
  [done] gt-abc.2: Scaffold  
  [done] gt-abc.3: Implement
  [current] gt-abc.4: Write tests [in_progress] <- YOU ARE HERE
  [pending] gt-abc.5: Documentation
  [pending] gt-abc.6: Exit decision

Progress: 3/6 steps complete

## Key behaviors
- Shows full molecule structure with status indicators
- Highlights current in_progress step
- If no in_progress, highlights first ready step
- Works without explicit cursor tracking (inferred from state)

## Implementation notes
- Query children of mol-id
- Sort by dependency order
- Find first in_progress or first ready
- Format with status indicators

## Gas Town integration
- gt-lz13: Update templates with nav workflow
- gt-um6q: Update docs with nav workflow","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-22T17:03:30.245964-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T17:36:31.936007-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T17:36:31.936007-08:00"}
